ucs.title: [LLM] TA577 NTLM hash theft via ZIP-delivered HTML coercing outbound SMB authentication
ucs.description: Hunts the February 2024 TA577 campaign that thread-hijacked replies to deliver ZIP archives containing HTML files which auto-referenced attacker-controlled SMB shares to coerce NTLMv2 authentication and capture Net-NTLMv2 hashes for relay/cracking. This is a TA577-signature primitive — they pivoted to it before pushing Pikabot/Latrodectus payloads.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name="*.html" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.htm") AND (Filesystem.file_path="*\\Temp\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Downloads\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*") AND (Filesystem.process_name="explorer.exe" OR Filesystem.process_name="7zG.exe" OR Filesystem.process_name="7zFM.exe" OR Filesystem.process_name="WinRAR.exe" OR Filesystem.process_name="Bandizip.exe") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename dest as host | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=true count as smb_count min(_time) as smb_firstTime max(_time) as smb_lastTime from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_port=445 AND NOT (All_Traffic.dest_ip=10.0.0.0/8 OR All_Traffic.dest_ip=172.16.0.0/12 OR All_Traffic.dest_ip=192.168.0.0/16 OR All_Traffic.dest_ip=100.64.0.0/10 OR All_Traffic.dest_ip=169.254.0.0/16) by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.dest_ip All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.app All_Traffic.user | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | rename src as host dest_ip as remote_smb_ip ] | where smb_firstTime >= firstTime AND smb_firstTime <= (lastTime + 600) | table firstTime host user file_name file_path process_name remote_smb_ip smb_count | sort - smb_count
ucs.kql:
let HtmlDrops = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FileName endswith ".html" or FileName endswith ".htm"
| where FolderPath has_any ("\\Temp\\", "\\Downloads\\", "\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("explorer.exe","7zG.exe","7zFM.exe","WinRAR.exe","Bandizip.exe","PeaZip.exe")
| project DropTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, InitiatingProcessFileName;
let ExternalSmb = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where RemotePort == 445 and ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess"
| where not(ipv4_is_private(RemoteIP))
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("explorer.exe","msedge.exe","chrome.exe","firefox.exe","iexplore.exe","OUTLOOK.EXE")
| project SmbTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, AccountName=InitiatingProcessAccountName;
HtmlDrops
| join kind=inner ExternalSmb on DeviceId
| where SmbTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + 10m)
| project DeviceName, AccountName, DropTime, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, SmbTime, RemoteIP, InitiatingProcessFileName1, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| sort by SmbTime desc
ucs.rationale: Outbound SMB (TCP/445) to a non-RFC1918 IP from a workstation is already rare in any sane environment; correlating it within 10 minutes of an HTML/HTM file being extracted from an archive in Temp/Downloads is the exact signature Proofpoint and Microsoft documented for the TA577 Feb-2024 NTLM-leak campaign. Generic Forced-Authentication detections fire on any HTML — this version anchors specificall

ucs.title: [LLM] TA577 Latrodectus / Pikabot loader chain: JS → curl/msiexec → rundll32 with named DLL export
ucs.description: Detects TA577's post-March-2024 loader sequence used to stage Latrodectus (and prior Pikabot): a thread-hijacked URL drops a JS/JSE that spawns curl.exe or msiexec.exe to fetch an MSI, which sideloads a DLL executed via rundll32 with the loader's signature export ordinal/name (e.g. 'homq', 'nail', 'AnselmoLaPastina', 'Crash' for Pikabot). Chains the IAB's known initial-access → execution → install pattern.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmd values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd values(Processes.process_name) as proc from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("curl.exe","msiexec.exe","powershell.exe")) OR (Processes.parent_process_name="msiexec.exe" AND Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe") OR (Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" AND (Processes.process="*homq*" OR Processes.process="*nail*" OR Processes.process="*AnselmoLaPastina*" OR Processes.process="*Crash*" OR Processes.process="*StartW*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval stage=case(parent_process_name IN ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe") AND process_name="curl.exe","1_js_to_curl", parent_process_name IN ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe") AND process_name="msiexec.exe","1_js_to_msi", parent_process_name="msiexec.exe" AND process_name="rundll32.exe","2_msi_to_rundll32", process_name="rundll32.exe" AND (match(process,"(?i)homq|nail|AnselmoLaPastina|Crash|StartW")),"3_rundll32_named_export", true(),"other") | stats count dc(stage) as stage_count values(stage) as stages values(process) as processes values(user) as user min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime by dest | where stage_count >= 2 | sort - stage_count
ucs.kql:
let Window = 30m;
let Stage1 = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe")
| where FileName in~ ("curl.exe","msiexec.exe","powershell.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("http://","https://",".msi","-o ","--output")
| project S1=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, S1Cmd=ProcessCommandLine, S1Proc=FileName, S1Parent=InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
let Stage2 = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "msiexec.exe"
| where FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
| project S2=Timestamp, DeviceId, S2Cmd=ProcessCommandLine;
let Stage3 = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\b(homq|nail|AnselmoLaPastina|Crash|StartW)\b"
   or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("\\AppData\\","\\ProgramData\\","\\Users\\Public\\","\\Temp\\")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (".dll,",".tmp,",",#1",",#2")
| project S3=Timestamp, DeviceId, S3Cmd=ProcessCommandLine;
Stage1
| join kind=inner Stage2 on DeviceId
| where S2 between (S1 .. S1 + Window)
| join kind=inner Stage3 on DeviceId
| where S3 between (S2 .. S2 + Window)
| project DeviceName, S1, S1Proc, S1Cmd, S2Cmd, S3, S3Cmd
| sort by S3 desc
ucs.rationale: wscript/cscript spawning curl.exe is virtually unheard-of outside malware loaders, and chaining it through msiexec → rundll32 with one of the documented Latrodectus/Pikabot export names ('homq', 'nail', 'AnselmoLaPastina', 'Crash', 'StartW') is the exact loader pattern Proofpoint, Elastic, and Walmart Cyber Intel reported for TA577's 2023-2024 distribution waves. Generic 'rundll32 from AppData' de
